description:
You are a SOC analyst investigating a web application breach. A packet capture /home/ga/Documents/captures/incident_capture.pcapng is already open in Wireshark. The capture contains traffic from multiple hosts, including both legitimate users and an attacker who compromised the web application and then exfiltrated data to a separate server.

Investigate the full attack chain and save your findings to /home/ga/Documents/incident_report.txt using exactly this format:

INCIDENT REPORT
================
ATTACKER_IP: <source IP of the attacker>
WEBSERVER_IP: <IP of the compromised web application server>
FAILED_LOGINS: <number of failed login attempts before the successful one>
VALID_CREDENTIALS: <username:password from the successful login>
WEBSHELL_FILENAME: <name of the malicious file uploaded by the attacker>
COMMANDS_EXECUTED: <comma-separated list of OS commands run via the webshell, in chronological order>
EXFIL_SERVER_IP: <IP of the server that received the stolen data>
EXFIL_BYTES: <total application-layer bytes sent from the attacker to the exfil server>

All values must come directly from the packet data. Examine every protocol layer carefully.
